author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
govardhana girivara, kevala nirjana sthala,
rāi kānu karibe śayane
lalitā⋅viśākhā saṅge, sevana kariba raṅge,
sukhamaya rātula caraṇe

kanaka sampūṭa kari, karpūra tāmbula bhari,
jogāiba vadanakamale
maṇimaya kiṅkiṇī, ratananūpura āni’,
parāiba caraṇayugale

kanaka kaṭorā pūri’, sugandhi candana buri,
dohākāra śrīaṅge ḍhāliba
gururūpā sakhā bāme, tribhaṅga bhaṅgima ṭhāme,
cāmarera vātāsa kariba

dohāra kamala ā̐khi, pulaka hoiyā dekhi’,
duhu̐ pada paraśiba kare
caitanya-dāsera dāsa, mane mātra abhilāṣa,
narottama⋅dāse sadā sphūre
en_translation:
Govardhana is the best of hills, a completely solitary place; Rāi and Kānu will be lying down. Along with Lalitā and Viśākhā, I will serve joyfully at Their blissful reddish feet.

Making a golden box, filling it with camphor and betel nuts, I will supply it to Their lotus mouths. Bringing a jeweled girdle and jeweled anklets, I will put them on Their feet.

Filling a golden cup, making a paste of fragrant sandalwood, I will pour it on the beautiful bodies of Both. With the guru-rūpā sakhī on the left, I will fan Them with a whisk in Their three-fold bending posture.

Seeing the lotus eyes of Both with hairs standing on end, I will touch Both of Their feet with my hands. The servant of the servant of Caitanya—this only desire in the mind always manifests in Narottama Dāsa.
